While covariance has been well understood in Euclidean spaces, it is ill-defined for random objects residing on nonlinear Riemannian manifolds, which...

items.title: Unbiased Derivative Estimation for Stationary Mean of Parameterized Markov chains
items.summary: arXiv:2606.11487v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We propose a new approach to unbiased estimation of the gradients of the stationary means associated with parametrized families of Markov chains. Our estimators are particularly efficient when the Markov chains have slow mixing rate. Our approach does not require a specific parametrization except for an...

items.title: Time Series Analysis in Machine Learning
items.summary: arXiv:2606.11746v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Time series analysis is a fundamental component of machine learning, especially in astrophysics and cosmology where temporal data abound. This chapter provides a pedagogical review of time series analysis techniques from a machine learning perspective. We cover the basic concepts of time series...

items.title: Accurate Estimation of Mutual Information in High Dimensional Data
items.summary: arXiv:2506.00330v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Mutual information (MI) quantifies statistical dependence between variables and is widely used across scientific disciplines, yet accurate estimation from finite data remains notoriously difficult.
